title: Lathyrus nudicaulis
taxonomy: Lathyrus nudicaulis is a species of flowering plant in the pea family, Fabaceae. It is native to North America. This plant is classified as a perennial herbaceous plant and its scientific classification is Lathyrus nudicaulis. The genus Lathyrus belongs to the tribe Loteae, within the subfamily Faboideae. Lathyrus nudicaulis is further classified as a member of the subgenus Lathyrus.
uses: Lathyrus nudicaulis is a plant species with reported traditional uses in medicine. Its roots and seeds have been used in folk remedies to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and skin problems. The plant's extracts may also have antimicrobial properties, which could aid in wound healing. However, more research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal potential and potential risks. Further studies are necessary to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human use.

side_effects: Lathyrus nudicaulis, also known as the hairy vetchling or narrow-leaved vetch, is not commonly discussed regarding its potential side effects, but in general, Lathyrus species may cause adverse effects when consumed in large quantities, including gastrointestinal issues, headaches, and allergic reactions. The plant contains a toxic compound called ODAP, which can cause neurolathyrism, a condition that affects the nervous system, but this is primarily associated with the consumption of Lathyrus sativus, not Lathyrus nudicaulis. However, more research is needed to fully understand the potential side effects of Lathyrus nudicaulis. intro: Lathyrus nudicaulis, also known as the winter pea or small pea, is a perennial legume native to North America. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its ability to support cardiovascular health and reduce inflammation due to its high content of fiber, vitamins, and minerals. The bioactive constituents of Lathyrus nudicaulis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, tinctures, and capsules, which are often used to support digestive and cardiovascular health.
